objective: Implement one dependency-free bounded lexical projection for only the channel_count and nominal_srate values borrowed through StreamInfoStaticFields, reproducing the exact seven accepted LSLC-001H numeric spellings without constructing XML, assigning runtime fields, or adding protocol, transport, adapter, provider, device, or authority behavior.
outputs: A private stream_info_static_numeric_spellings module exporting a bounded public projection over one borrowed StreamInfoStaticFields view, with explicit accessors for channel-count and nominal-rate text and no XML node or document ownership.
Exact deterministic spellings for the seven accepted cases: channel counts 1 through 7; irregular rate 0.000000000000000; and the accepted regular spellings for 1, 59.94, 100, 256.5, and 1000000.25.
Typed rejection for regular rates outside the explicitly evidenced fixed-decimal domain instead of silently inventing exponent, precision, rounding, locale, or canonicalization policy.

A separate LSLC-001L local-results overlay and focused checker that bind the accepted K semantic projection and H observations while leaving all H raw/public XML, volatile fields, description hierarchy, and full-document candidate status unchanged.

acceptance.proof: The projection borrows accepted K state, allocates only bounded owned numeric text after explicit reserve checks, never mutates or consumes its source, and exposes no XML or runtime-field surface.

All seven accepted case inputs execute directly in Rust and produce the exact observed channel_count and nominal_srate spellings, including the two irregular cases and every recorded regular-rate precision.
Values outside the explicitly accepted fixed-decimal domain return stable typed errors and do not fall back to exponent, shortest-round-trip, locale-sensitive, truncated, or silently rounded output.

The new local overlay binds immutable H and K artifacts and records only the two numeric lexical candidate fields; public observed XML, runtime placeholders, desc mapping, and full-document candidate_result remain unchanged.
Cargo files, locks, dependencies, features, unsafe usage, XML assembly, runtime effects, adapters, providers, devices, and Manifold authority remain unchanged.
Public instructions route the bounded numeric lexical surface and its no-XML/no-runtime limits; shared skills are reviewed; the portable workspace remains fail-closed with no device requirement.

commit_policy: Commit the bounded static numeric lexical projection separately from XML elements, complete documents, description mapping, volatile/runtime fields, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, features, or external integration.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the observed two-field numeric lexical projection, fail-closed domain, and strict no-XML/no-runtime/no-authority boundary.
Describe the bounded numeric spelling surface, exact observed cases, typed unsupported domain, and remaining XML/runtime work.
Review whether the fail-closed lexical-policy boundary adds reusable authority guidance; retain the skill unchanged if current rules cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether the clean Rusty LSL lexical adapter needs new repo-family routing; retain the skill unchanged if current public/source/Manifold boundaries cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, morphospace/feature.lock.json, morphospace/project.spec.json, existing accepted fixtures, oracle drivers, raw captures, H/K observations or provenance, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, generated protocol material, or native libraries.

XML validation, escaping, names, elements, trees, serialization, declaration, whitespace, desc-root interpretation, complete info documents, endpoint bytes, parsing, decoding, round trips, canonicalization, or mutation.
Version, created_at, uid, session_id, hostname, addresses, ports, or any other volatile/runtime-populated or transport-owned field.

Broad floating-point lexical compatibility outside the seven accepted values; locale policy; exponent policy; shortest-round-trip policy; NaN, infinity, negative, subnormal, or zero regular-rate behavior; or claims beyond the explicitly tested fixed-decimal domain.

Oracle execution, network access, source-repository implementation inspection, or use of liblsl, rLSL, pylsl, wrapper, application, build, vendored, generated, protocol, or test source as implementation input.

Discovery, resolution, inlet, outlet, samples, chunks, buffering, queues, clocks, correction, recovery, protocol, wire, sockets, networking, runtime activation, FFI, adapters, providers, devices, Quest, Hostess, or platform lifecycle.
Manifold stream admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, or product-policy authority.
